title: The Old Man (The Adventures Of Seumas Beg)
content:
An old man sat beneath a tree
            Alone;
        So still was he
            That, if he had been carved in stone,
        He could not be
            More quiet or more cold:
        He was an ancient man
            More than
        A thousand ages old.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Stephens, titled "The Old Man (The Adventures Of Seumas Beg)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
